question: Based on this 100% stacked bar chart for the platform, what can we conclude about the quarterly number of 'Complaints Received'?

options.A: The chart shows only the proportion of complaints each quarter, so we cannot determine whether the absolute number of complaints increased or decreased over the year.
options.B: The absolute number of complaints received increases each quarter because the complaints segment takes up a larger share in later quarters.
options.C: The absolute number of complaints received is the same in every quarter because all bars reach 100%.
options.D: The absolute number of complaints received is lowest in Q3 because its segment is smallest there.

explanation: The chart normalizes unrelated categories to 100% within each quarter. It can suggest that the share of a metric is higher in later quarters, but it cannot establish an absolute increasing trend without the underlying totals. A higher percentage share does not necessarily mean a higher absolute quantity.
